title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 59.
content:
There was an Old Man of Corfu,
    Who never knew what he should do;
    So he rushed up and down,
    Till the sun made him brown,
    That bewildered Old Man of Corfu.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 59."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
